body_txt: Often in fiction, Ms. Fanservice will be depicted in a pose with her back to the audience, but turning at the waist and often looking over her shoulder in the process. It's a common pose for a few reasons: While this pose is possible to achieve in multiple variations in Real Life , its use in non-live action media favors exaggerated versions that are at best painful and unlikely to occur in natural motion and at worst defy physics and anatomy. For instance, the character's torso may almost point backward or the character will be sticking her butt out and curving her spine vertically rather than merely twisting sideways. This pose is a staple of Fanservice that goes way back and had its turn at scrutiny in 2012 when a large-scale discussion about it spread across the internet. Many see framing a woman with her butt and breasts facing the viewer as objectifying and as prioritizing sex appeal over badassery, especially since this is by and large a women's only trope. Compare Contrapposto Pose , Head-and-Hip Pose , Coy, Girlish Flirt Pose and Leg Cling . Also may be combined with Dynamic Akimbo , which is typically associated with manliness, which is why women may get framed with this trope so that they still look sexy.
